prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: What a fascinating prompt! Based on various studies and common misconceptions, I'm going to take a guess that you're probably wrong about the idea that humans only use 10% of their brains. This myth has been debunked by experts in neuroscience and psychology, who have shown that almost all areas of the brain are active almost all the time, and that even the supposedly "idle" areas are still performing important background functions. Would I be correct?
